assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address they can verify.
No GitHub personal account, hello-world repository, branch, commit, or pull request exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a GitHub account and complete the Hello World browser workflow through a merged pull request that publishes a README change on main.

first_success.outcome: The hello-world repository main branch shows the committed README change after the pull request is merged.
first_success.observable_signal: GitHub reports the pull request successfully merged and closed, and the hello-world Code tab shows the README change on main.

route_selection.surface: GitHub.com personal signup plus Hello World web tutorial
route_selection.rule: Prefer the documented Hello World browser path over CLI or REST API first miles, and resolve signup method only when post-submit verification and anti-abuse screens are established.

candidates.name: Email and password personal signup, then Hello World
candidates.reason: Public signup shows Email, Password, Username, and Country/Region, and Hello World is fully documented, but current docs only say follow the prompts and verify email without fixing anti-abuse or verification UI.

candidates.name: Continue with Google or Continue with Apple, then Hello World
candidates.reason: Peer social buttons sit on the signup page; provider consent screens and whether they change email verification are not established as shorter or equivalent.

candidates.name: GitHub CLI quickstart or REST API getting started
candidates.reason: Official start-your-journey materials present Hello World as the browser essentials path; CLI and REST API are different surfaces and outcomes.

required_path.action: Create a free personal GitHub account from github.com/signup.

required_fields.label: Email verification and any anti-abuse challenge
required_fields.notes: Docs require email verification before some basic tasks such as creating a repository, but do not document the exact verification control or whether a CAPTCHA or puzzle appears.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a verified personal GitHub account that can create repositories.

required_path.action: Create the hello-world repository with a README.

required_fields.label: Repository name
required_fields.notes: Tutorial sets the name to hello-world.

required_path.observable_result: The hello-world repository exists with a README on main.

required_path.action: Create the readme-edits branch from main.
required_path.observable_result: The readme-edits branch is available in the repository.

required_path.action: Edit README.md on readme-edits and commit the change.
required_path.observable_result: A commit with the README edit exists on readme-edits.

required_path.action: Open a pull request from readme-edits into main and create it.
required_path.observable_result: An open pull request compares readme-edits with main.

required_path.action: Merge the pull request and confirm the merge.
required_path.observable_result: GitHub reports the pull request successfully merged and closed.

required_path.action: Open the hello-world Code tab and confirm the README change is on main.
required_path.observable_result: The Code tab shows the committed README change on main.

prerequisites.description: A reachable email address is required so GitHub can complete account verification.

external_gates.description: Email verification must succeed before the account can complete some basic tasks such as creating a repository.

unavoidable_waits.description: GitHub must deliver the signup email verification before the developer can finish verification.

platform_outcomes.description: GitHub creates the personal account after signup prompts are completed.
Merging the pull request updates main with the README commit.

excluded.item: Deleting the readme-edits branch after merge
excluded.reason: The first-success signal is the merged README on main; branch deletion is cleanup after that signal is available.
Official account docs recommend 2FA but present it as a next step after account creation, not a Hello World prerequisite.

excluded.item: GitHub CLI or REST API quickstarts
excluded.reason: Different interaction surfaces from the selected Hello World browser workflow.

sources.title: Hello World
evidence_supported: Repository, branch, commit, pull request, and merge steps

sources.title: Creating an account on GitHub
evidence_supported: Google and Apple social login alternatives

sources.title: GitHub signup
evidence_supported: Email, Password, Username, and Country/Region fields
Continue with Google and Continue with Apple

sources.title: Getting started with the REST API
evidence_supported: REST API as an alternate developer surface

uncertainties.question: Which signup method and post-submit verification or anti-abuse screens does a fresh personal account currently hit before Hello World can start?
uncertainties.impact: Email versus social signup and any CAPTCHA, code, or puzzle steps change required fields and whether the account action can be marked verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough from github.com/signup through verified email and first repository creation, capturing every required control.

uncertainties.question: Should the corpus first mile be Hello World collaboration basics or a developer REST API path?
uncertainties.impact: API credentials and first request work are a different goal and action sequence from the browser pull-request tutorial.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An explicit workshop goal choice between Hello World and the REST API getting-started route.
